title: Chicken Fried Rice

ingredients.title: For the chicken:
items: 250g (½ lb) boneless chicken breast, diced small

ingredients.title: For the rice:
items: 3 cups cooked jasmine rice (preferably cold, day-old)
3-4 spring onions, chopped (reserve some green parts for garnish)

ingredients.title: Sauce & Seasoning:
items: Optional: a pinch of sugar for balance

instruction.title: Prepare the Chicken:
steps: In a small bowl, mix diced chicken with soy sauce, cornstarch, sesame oil, and white pepper. Let marinate for 10–15 minutes. This ensures tender, flavorful chicken.

instruction.title: Prepare the Rice:
steps: Cold, day-old rice works best. Break up any clumps so each grain is separate.

instruction.title: Cook the Eggs:
steps: Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat.
Add beaten eggs, stir quickly to scramble lightly, then remove from pan. Set aside.

instruction.title: Cook the Chicken:
steps: Heat 1 tbsp oil in the same wok over high heat.
Stir-fry chicken until just cooked through (about 3–4 minutes). Remove and set aside.

instruction.title: Vegetables & Aromatics:
steps: Add a little more oil if needed.
Stir-fry onion, garlic, and carrots until aromatic and slightly softened (2–3 minutes).
Toss in peas and cook another 1 minute.

instruction.title: Fry the Rice:
steps: Add cold rice to the wok.
Stir-fry, breaking up clumps, ensuring rice is coated with oil and heated evenly.
Return chicken and scrambled eggs to the pan.

instruction.title: Season the Rice:
steps: Add soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, white pepper, and optional sugar.
Toss thoroughly to combine. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Stir in spring onion whites, reserving green parts for garnish.

instruction.title: Finish & Serve:
steps: Garnish with green parts of spring onion.
Serve hot in a bowl or plate with a slight drizzle of sesame oil for aroma.

chefTips: Rice Texture: Use day-old rice. Fresh rice is too soft and becomes mushy.
High Heat: Use a wok over high heat to get that “wok hei” smoky flavor.
Oil Distribution: Coat rice grains evenly with oil to prevent sticking and to achieve a slightly glossy look.
Layering Flavors: Cook chicken, eggs, and vegetables separately, then combine, to maintain distinct textures.
Optional Extras: Add a few drops of soy sauce for color or a dash of Shaoxing wine while stir-frying for depth.
